Welcome to Wave Terminal

Wave is an open-source terminal that combines traditional terminal features with graphical capabilities like file previews, web browsing, and AI assistance. It runs on MacOS, Linux, and Windows.

Modern development involves constantly switching between terminals and browsers - checking documentation, previewing files, monitoring systems, and using AI tools. Wave brings these graphical tools directly into the terminal, letting you control them from the command line. This means you can stay in your terminal workflow while still having access to the visual interfaces you need.

Check out Getting Started for installation instructions.

Wave AI
Context-aware terminal assistant with access to terminal output, widgets, and filesystem.
Customization
Set up tabs and terminals to match your workflow needs.
Key Bindings
Boost efficiency with keyboard shortcuts for faster navigation.
Layout
Organize your workspace using our layout system.
Remote Connections
Quickly SSH or connect to remote machines in one step.
Widgets
Explore built-in tools to extend your terminal’s functionality.
wsh Command
Control Wave and launch widgets directly from the command line.
info

If you have a question, please feel free to ask us in Discord. If you'd like to file a bug/enchancement, please use Github Issues. These docs are also open-source and we do accept PRs for docs here. You can click the "Edit this page" link at the bottom of the page to get taken directly to the editor page for that document in GitHub.

Roadmap​

Want to provide input to our future releases? Connect with us on Discord or open a Feature Request!
